title: fix "podman -h" help output
body:
`podman -h` currently returns an error:
`Error: pflag: help requested`

This bug was introduced in 44d037898ebc, the problem is that we wrap the
error and cobra lib checks with `==` for this one and not errors.Is().
I have a PR upstream to fix this but for now this also works.

Signed-off-by: Paul Holzinger <pholzing@redhat.com>

<!--

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Fixed a bug where `podman -h` did not show the help output.
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Test now fails:
```
  #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv                                                                                                         
   #| FAIL: podman container rename: --help has 'Options:' section but no '[options]' in synopsis                                                               
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^                    
```
Reason:
```
Options:                                                                                                                                                        
  -h, --help   help for rename                                                                                                                                  
 ```
This is not easy to fix.

Ah I see, I can fix it in a different way in the go code.
Looks like we need this special flag defined then, the comment did not reflect the reason for this so I thought I can remove it.

Thanks. If it turns out not to be easy to fix, just say so, I had an idea on a possible workaround. (As a matter of aesthetics I object slightly to documenting `--help` as an _option_, because I interpret _option_ as something that applies to actual functionality... but I won't push on this)
@edsantiago I am sure that this will fix it.

Yep, this is perfect. Thank you.

/lgtm
/hold

This was blocked for an hour-plus because of a weird CI issue. Filed #14623.

/hold cancel
